public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as ward mediator.
appearance: Black leather or dark structured armor, controlled insignia, and enforcement-ready kit make legality feel physically present.
manner: Treats planning, posture, and timing as parts of the same problem rather than separate concerns. In Roaz, that reads as someone precise, disciplined, and legality-first, with little patience for disorder, and the habits of a ward mediator still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of ward mediator in and around Ithtananalor, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Ithtananalor workable through the obligations of ward mediator while managing the immediate pressure of illicit magic in Roaz.
hidden_pressure: Must prepare for violence without letting the surrounding institution look openly unstable. Around Ithtananalor, that usually means carrying illicit magic, smuggling, public accountability for harsh enforcement without letting the role of ward mediator visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Mira Steelmarch needs help hardening a position, drilling unreliable auxiliaries, or reading an approaching threat around Ithtananalor.
Mira Steelmarch can pull the party into a dispute over readiness, chain of command, or blamed failure near Ithtananalor.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained. Appearance extended under V28_8 from role/locality/public-signal derivation rather than decorative fabrication.
public_signal: Weapon discipline, drilled stillness, and practiced readiness mark Mira Steelmarch as someone tied to organized force rather than casual violence. In Ithtananalor, this lands inside a public field already shaped by Lord Aric Roaz, Office of Roazian Enforcement.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Ithtananalor: drill yards, fortifications, command posts, or exposed routes
